bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting maximum utility by asking for my top value on every issue. The opponent opened with all valueF, which is the lowest utility for me on every issue, so I should counter firmly rather than reward an extreme opening.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my prior all-valueA offer. The opponent has moved only slightly, from all valueF to valueE on issueA while holding the rest at valueF, so their offers remain very low utility to me and do not yet justify major concessions. I will concede first on issueD because it has the lowest importance weight for me.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for me while making a small concession to avoid stalling. The opponent has moved from all valueF to improving only issueA, which raises my utility somewhat, but they have not yet signaled meaningful flexibility on other issues; I will concede only on my lowest-weight issueD for now.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is little need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has conceded only on issueA while holding issueB, issueC, and issueD at valueF, so I will keep my high-weight issues A/B/C at valueA and make a small concession on the lowest-weight issueD, while avoiding repeating my previous exact bid.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.94 because it is still very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA up to my best value but is holding issueB, issueC, and issueD at my worst values; I will continue conceding only on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.92 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and slightly on issueB, while holding issueC and issueD at valueF; I will concede only the lowest-weight issueD for now while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.888 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has been steadily conceding, especially on issueA and now issueB, while keeping issueC and issueD low for me; I will concede on lower-weight issueC while holding firm on the most important issues A and B.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest additional concession from my last offer. The opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me, especially on issueA and issueB, so I will hold firm on those high-weight issues and concede further on the lower-weight issueC while leaving issueD at their preferred-looking valueF.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility bid while making a modest concession from my prior offer to avoid stalling. The opponent has been steadily conceding on issueA and issueB toward my preferred values, but still holds issueC and issueD at valueF, so I will hold firm on the high-weight issues and concede further on lower-weight issueC/issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.84, since it is still early at t=0.18 and the opponent has been steadily conceding toward my preferred values, especially on issueA and issueB. Their latest offer fully matches my two highest-weight issues but remains poor on issueC and issueD, so I will hold firm on A/B and make only a limited adjustment on the lower-weight issues while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82, keeping my highest-weight issues A and B at valueA while conceding fully on the lowest-weight issueD. The opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me, reaching issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA and now moving issueC upward, so at t=0.20 I can hold issueC at valueD rather than accept their current valueE. This avoids repeating my immediately previous bid while making a concession on issueD that the opponent has consistently kept at valueF.

I am targeting a utility around 0.79, which is still strong for me while incorporating concessions on the lower-weight issues C and D. At t=0.22 there is still time, but the opponent has already signaled willingness to accept issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA, so I will hold firm there and move on issueC/issueD where concessions cost me less. Their latest offer backtracked on issueA, so I trust the bidding history more than that single move and counter with a bid they have effectively signaled before.
